title: Core Business Dimensions in a Data Warehouse
description: A visual breakdown of the fundamental components that make up a business dimension, which are descriptive categories used for filtering, grouping, and labeling data in a data warehouse.
elements: Slowly Changing Dimension (SCD) Types (I, II, III)
relationships: The Dimension Table is the central element, containing a unique Dimension Key and multiple descriptive Attributes. These Attributes can be organized into Hierarchies for drill-down analysis. The SCD Types define how changes to these Attributes are managed over time.
